name: Oat Porridge

book_claims.claim: Suitable whole grain breakfast food in a longevity diet
book_claims.mechanism: Provides slow-releasing carbohydrates and soluble fibre for sustained energy and satiety

supplementary_research.finding: A 2016 meta-analysis (Br J Nutr, PMID 27724985) of 28 RCTs found that oat-based breakfasts significantly increased satiety and reduced energy intake at subsequent meals compared to other breakfast cereals, mediated by beta-glucan viscosity.

A 2015 RCT (J Am Coll Nutr, PMID 25411276) found that oatmeal porridge produced greater satiety, reduced hunger, and lower desire to eat compared to ready-to-eat oat-based cereal, despite equal calories, attributed to beta-glucan's viscosity.

A 2016 systematic review (Nutr Rev, PMID 26690472) confirmed that oat beta-glucan at doses of 3g or more per day reduces LDL cholesterol by 5-10%, with porridge being the most studied delivery format.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic porridge topping; anthocyanin-rich fruit adds antioxidant benefit
Omega-3 rich nuts add healthy fats and protein to grain base
Warming spice with blood sugar-stabilizing properties
Natural sweetener with potassium; creamy texture complement
Omega-3 and lignans add anti-inflammatory benefit

seasonality: Year-round pantry staple. Steel-cut oats take longest to cook (20-30 min) but retain most texture and lowest GI. Rolled oats cook in 5 minutes. Instant oats have highest GI.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 2.3-8.5 g (dry oats); a typical 40g serving provides 1.5-3.4g
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Soluble fiber that forms viscous gel in the gut. Cooking increases viscosity and effectiveness. Steel-cut and rolled oats retain more beta-glucan structure than instant.
Unique polyphenols found only in oats. Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant; may reduce blood pressure via increased nitric oxide production.
Exceptionally high content. Cofactor for superoxide dismutase antioxidant enzyme.
Important for bone health. Partially bound by phytic acid; soaking overnight improves bioavailability.
Non-heme iron; absorption enhanced by adding vitamin C-rich fruits like berries.

synergies.description: Combined at breakfast with yogurt, nuts and fruit for sustained energy and complete nutrition
Vitamin C from berries enhances iron absorption from oats; antioxidants complement beta-glucan's cardiovascular benefits
